content_body:
While hotel rooms have their merit, there are just some areas where they fall short. When it comes to making memories with friends and family, you're probably not going to remember that room that looked just like every other hotel room you've stayed in. But that time you went on that mountain retreat and stayed in a luxurious cabin that felt like home? That you'll remember—because it was just a better experience. Here are seven reasons why:

 

1. Space

It goes without saying that renting a home will give you more space than renting a hotel room. Instead of trying to cram the whole family into a queen bed and a rollout cot, enjoy spacious living areas, open kitchens, and separate bedrooms. Enjoy spending time together, but not feeling like you're on top of each other.

 

2. Porches

Hotels sometimes provide small balconies with some outdoor furniture if you're lucky, but nothing beats a full covered deck overlooking a rushing river, a peaceful lake, or gorgeous mountain views. Relaxing on a nice, open deck is one of the best ways to relax and rejuvenate. And isn't that what vacationing is all about?

 

3. Full Kitchens

Preparing meals just isn't the same when all you have is a microwave and a mini fridge. In our luxury cabins, you'll find fully stocked kitchens with everything you need to cook even the most elaborate of meals—even the dishes!

 

4. Al Fresco Dining

Now that you've prepared a tasty meal in the fully equipped kitchen, nothing beats enjoying it while breathing in the fresh mountain air. The great views don't hurt either.

 

5. Privacy

Feel like singing at the top of your lungs? Kids running around making more noise than you thought was humanly possible? We're not going to complain! And neither will your neighbors because, well, you won't have any.

 

6. Fireplaces

A crucial part of any cabin experience, a roaring fire makes the place feel like home. Unfortunately, most hotels can't offer enormous stone fireplaces for cozying up with loved ones. Luckily, cabins do.

 

7. Nature

Sure, city lights have their appeal, but they've got nothing on the beauty of Mother Nature. Walking out the back door and finding this just a few feet from your cabin will make you wonder why you ever stayed in a hotel in the first place.
title: 7 Reasons Cabins Are Better Than Hotels
